sn22.76:1.2: There the Blessed One addressed the monks, “Monks, form is inconstant.
sn22.76:1.5: That is to be seen with right discernment as it has come to be: ‘This is not mine. This is not my self. This is not what I am.’
sn22.76:1.12: That is to be seen with right discernment as it has come to be: ‘This is not mine. This is not my self. This is not what I am.’
sn22.76:2.1: “Seeing thus, the instructed disciple of the noble ones grows disenchanted with form, disenchanted with feeling, disenchanted with perception, disenchanted with fabrications, disenchanted with consciousness.
sn22.76:2.2: Disenchanted, he becomes dispassionate. Through dispassion, he is released.
sn22.76:2.3: With release, there is the knowledge, ‘Released.’ He discerns that ‘Birth is ended, the holy life fulfilled, the task done. There is nothing further for this world.’
sn22.76:2.4: “Monks, to whatever extent there are abodes of beings, to whatever extent there is the height of becoming, arahants are supreme, arahants are the best in the world.”
sn22.76:3.1: That is what the Blessed One said.
sn22.76:3.2: Having said that, the One Well-Gone, the Teacher, said further:
sn22.76:4.3: Cut off is the conceit, ‘I am’;
sn22.76:6.4: They are the Buddha’s children, his sons.
sn22.76:8.3: they are the best in the world:
sn22.76:9.1: The adept’s knowledge has arisen in them:
sn22.76:9.3: As for the essence of the holy life,
sn22.76:9.4: they don’t depend on others in that.
sn22.76:10.3: Having reached the level of the tamed,7
sn22.76:10.4: they have victory in the world.
sn22.76:11.4: ‘In the world, the awakened are unexcelled!’”
